avalancheActivity.comment:
On very steep sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation, especially at the base of rock walls.
Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early. Very early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the near-surface layers during the night. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes. The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.

In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In particular below approximately 2500 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The weather conditions fostered a substantial stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. These can only be released by large loads in most cases. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early. Very early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be sunny. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. In addition, only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
The spring-like weather conditions in the late morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m.
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible from the late morning, even large ones in isolated cases. In particular on steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.

Early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise in the late morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.
